senses.definition: to have a specific flavor

examples.x: The soup tastes delicious with the added herbs.
Coffee always tastes better when freshly brewed.
Yesterday, the pasta dish she made tasted a bit too salty.
The cake they baked last week tasted amazing.
The sauce tasted of tangy tomatoes and garlic, perfect for pasta.
The wine tasted of ripe berries and oak, with a smooth finish.
The pastry tasted of flaky butter and sweet cinnamon, melting in your mouth.

senses.definition: meat from a pig, eaten as food

examples.x: He enjoyed a hearty breakfast of bacon, eggs, and sausage made from pork.
The barbecue restaurant specializes in slow-smoked pork ribs with a tangy barbecue sauce.
The recipe called for marinating the pork chops in a mixture of soy sauce, garlic, and ginger before grilling.

senses.definition: meat that is from a cow

examples.x: The roast beef sandwich at the deli is a customer favorite, piled high with thinly sliced beef and horseradish sauce.
The recipe called for braising beef short ribs in red wine until they were fall-off-the-bone tender.
She ordered a rare steak, preferring her beef to be cooked just enough to seal in the juices.

senses.definition: meat that is from a young sheep

examples.x: The Easter celebration includes a traditional dish of roasted lamb, symbolizing rebirth and renewal.
The Middle Eastern dish, shawarma, features thinly sliced lamb cooked on a vertical rotisserie.
The shepherd's pie is made with minced lamb, topped with creamy mashed potatoes.

senses.definition: the meat of a large fish named tuna that lives in warm waters

examples.x: She made a tuna sandwich for lunch.
The chef served fresh tuna steaks with a side of vegetables.
The grilled tuna was seasoned with lemon and herbs.
She added chunks of tuna to her green salad.
The restaurant’s special was a seared tuna fillet.

senses.definition: a dish that consists of eggs mixed together and cooked in a frying pan

examples.x: She cooked an omelet using leftover vegetables and shredded cheddar cheese for a quick and easy dinner.
He learned how to flip an omelet without breaking it by practicing with a non-stick pan.

senses.definition: a book that explains how a dish is cooked

examples.x: She referred to her grandmother's old cookbook for traditional family recipes passed down through generations.
The bookstore has a wide selection of cookbooks, ranging from Italian cuisine to vegan cooking.
He gifted her a beginner's cookbook to help her learn the basics of cooking.
The celebrity chef released a new cookbook featuring his signature dishes and cooking techniques.
She bookmarked her favorite recipes in the cookbook for easy reference while meal planning.

senses.definition: (typically plural) food and other items, typically household goods, that we buy at a supermarket such as eggs, flour, etc.

examples.x: She made a list of groceries to buy for the weekend.
I need to pick up some groceries on my way home from work.
The store offers online delivery services for groceries.
After the shopping trip, they unpacked the groceries in the kitchen.

senses.definition: the additional money we give someone such as a waiter, driver, etc. to thank them for the services they have given us

examples.x: The hotel porter received a tip for carrying the luggage to the guests' room.
She always makes sure to leave a tip for the barista at her favorite coffee shop as a token of appreciation.
The taxi driver was grateful for the tip he received from the passengers for helping with their luggage.
He left a generous tip for the waiter who provided excellent service throughout the meal.
He forgot to leave a tip for the hairdresser after his haircut, so he went back to the salon to give it to her.

senses.definition: a part of something that is left

examples.x: After finishing her meal, she put the rest of the food in the refrigerator.
He drank most of the water and left the rest for his brother.
She used some of the money to pay bills and saved the rest for a vacation.
The teacher divided the candy among the students and kept the rest for herself.
The team completed most of the project, but the rest will have to be finished tomorrow.

senses.definition: the amount of food that remains uneaten after a meal and is typically saved for later consumption

examples.x: He made a delicious soup using the leftovers from last night's roast chicken.
She heated up the leftovers from yesterday's pasta dish for a quick and easy dinner.
The restaurant donates its excess food and leftovers to local shelters and charities.
He often gets creative in the kitchen, using leftovers to make new and exciting dishes.
They decided to order extra food so they would have plenty of leftovers to enjoy throughout the week.

senses.definition: a large piece of meat or fish cut into thick slices

examples.x: She seasoned the steak with salt and pepper before grilling it to perfection on the barbecue.
They enjoyed a romantic dinner at home, complete with candlelight and a perfectly cooked steak.
The butcher recommended a thick-cut steak for the barbecue, ensuring it stayed juicy and flavorful.
She served the grilled steak with a side of mashed potatoes and sautéed vegetables for a hearty meal.
He prefers his steak cooked rare, with a charred crust on the outside and a warm, red center.

senses.definition: (of meat) completely cooked in a way that there is not any pink flesh inside

examples.x: She prefers her steak well-done, with no trace of pinkness in the center.
He instructed the chef to cook his burger well-done to ensure it was thoroughly cooked.
The chicken breast was grilled until it was well-done and had a golden-brown crust on the outside.
She checked the pork chops with a meat thermometer to ensure they were cooked well-done before serving.
He asked the waiter to have his salmon cooked well-done, as he preferred it fully cooked.

senses.definition: (of meat) cooked in a way that there is only a small amount of pink flesh inside

examples.x: The chicken breast was grilled until it reached medium doneness, with a moist and tender texture.
The hamburger was grilled to medium, satisfyingly juicy with a hint of pinkness.
The lamb chops were cooked to medium, perfectly tender with a pink center, and served with mint sauce.

senses.definition: (of meat) cooked for a short time in a way that the flesh is still red inside

examples.x: He ordered his burger cooked rare, wanting it juicy with a red center.
She prefers her duck breast cooked rare, with the skin crispy and the meat tender.
The filet mignon was cooked rare, just enough to sear the outside while keeping the inside tender and juicy.
She enjoys her steak cooked rare, with a warm red center and a seared crust on the outside.
The restaurant specializes in rare cuts of premium-quality meat.

senses.definition: having too much water and little taste

examples.x: The soup was disappointingly watery, lacking the rich flavor of homemade broth.
He complained that the pasta sauce was too watery, with not enough thickness and depth of flavor.
She added too much liquid to the batter, resulting in a watery consistency for the pancake mix.
The coffee tasted watery, as if it had been brewed with too much water and not enough coffee grounds.
The smoothie was watery and bland, lacking the creaminess and sweetness of properly blended fruit.

senses.definition: having a strong taste that gives your mouth a pleasant burning feeling

examples.x: She enjoyed the spicy curry with its blend of aromatic spices that lingered on her palate.
He couldn't handle the spicy chili sauce and reached for a glass of water to cool his burning mouth.
They ordered the spicy Thai noodles, craving the intense heat and bold flavors.

senses.definition: having a strong taste that is unpleasant and not sweet

examples.x: The bitter taste of black coffee lingered on her tongue after she took a sip.
He made a face at the bitter medicine, finding it difficult to swallow.
He winced at the bitter aftertaste of the dark chocolate, preferring sweeter varieties.
The bitter flavor of the arugula salad was balanced by the sweetness of the balsamic dressing.
Despite its bitter taste, he appreciated the health benefits of eating kale in his salad.

senses.definition: someone who avoids eating meat

examples.x: She became a vegetarian after learning about the environmental and ethical implications of meat consumption.
The restaurant offers a variety of options for vegetarians, including salads, vegetable stir-fries, and tofu dishes.
He has been a vegetarian for years and enjoys experimenting with plant-based recipes at home.
As a vegetarian, she prefers to cook with plant-based ingredients such as tofu, beans, and lentils.
She has been a vegetarian for five years and feels healthier.

senses.definition: someone who does not consume or use anything that is produced from animals, such as meat, milk, or eggs

examples.x: She became a vegan after learning about the ethical and environmental impact of animal agriculture.
She has been a vegan for several years and advocates for animal rights and environmental sustainability.
He became a vegan after learning about the health benefits of a plant-based diet.
The vegans at the potluck brought a variety of delicious plant-based dishes to share with everyone.
The vegans in the group shared tips and recipes for making vegan versions of their favorite dishes.

senses.definition: a vegetable with a thick stem and clusters of edible flower buds, typically green in color

examples.x: He tried to mask the taste of the broccoli by adding extra seasoning and sauce.
She wrinkled her nose at the taste of broccoli.
Purple broccoli is less common but just as nutritious as green.
The market sells both green and purple broccoli fresh from the farm.

senses.definition: a green vegetable that people eat raw or use in cooking

examples.x: He dislikes the taste of celery and avoids it in his meals.
It is important to wash celery thoroughly before consuming it.
She includes thin slices of celery in her diet.

senses.definition: a vegetable with dark purple skin, which is eaten cooked

examples.x: The Mediterranean restaurant served baba ganoush, a creamy dip made from roasted eggplant, tahini, and garlic, with warm pita bread.
She roasted slices of eggplant in the oven with olive oil and herbs for a flavorful side dish.
They harvested fresh eggplants from the garden to use in ratatouille, a traditional French dish.
The stir-fry featured chunks of eggplant cooked with tofu, bell peppers, and a savory sauce.
He grilled whole eggplants on the barbecue until they were tender and smoky.

senses.definition: a large round vegetable with thick white, green or purple leaves, eaten raw or cooked

examples.x: She prepared a delicious coleslaw using fresh cabbage, carrots, and a tangy dressing.
The farmer harvested the cabbage crop just in time for the summer market.
Cabbage is a versatile vegetable that can be used in salads, soups, and stir-fries.
Fermented cabbage, known as sauerkraut, is popular in many cultures for its unique flavor and health benefits.
The recipe called for a head of cabbage, which was sautéed with garlic and spices for a flavorful side dish.

senses.definition: dark and wide green leaves of an Asian plant that can be eaten cooked or uncooked

examples.x: She added fresh spinach to the salad.
Spinach is a good source of iron and vitamins.
He cooked spinach with garlic and olive oil.
The soup contained spinach and other vegetables.
She blended spinach into her morning smoothie.

senses.definition: to give a small amount of money to a waiter, driver, etc. to thank them for their services

examples.x: She tipped the waitress generously for her attentive service during the dinner.
He always tips the taxi driver when he takes a ride to show appreciation for their promptness.
They tipped the tour guide at the end of the excursion for providing an informative and enjoyable experience.
The customers tipped the bartender after receiving their drinks promptly and with a smile.
She remembered to tip the delivery person when the food arrived hot and on time.
